rulings.Case Title: RISTEARD JOSEPH O'SUILLEABHAIN, et. al. VS. ALAC DEAN CONSTRUCTION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 7, 2011, line 1, DEFENDANTS UNION SQUARE INVESTMENTS, LLC, SABEER BHATIA TRUST, SABEER BHATIA'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO THE 1ST, 2ND AND 3RD CAUSES OF ACTION IS OVERRULED. WHEN TESTING A PLEADING AGAINST A DEMURRER, THE FACTS ALLEGED IN THE PLEADING ARE DEEMED TO BE TRUE, HOWEVER IMPROBABLE THEY ARE. DEL E. WEBB CORP. V. STRUCTURAL MATERIALS CO. (1981) 123 CAL.APP.3D 593, 604. THE LANGUAGE IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT INDICATES THAT A CONTRACTUAL RELATIONSHIP EXISTED BETWEEN PLAINTIFF AND DEFENDANTS. THEREFORE, THE CLAIMS ARE PERMISSIBLE. MOREOVER, THE PLAINTIFF HAS SUFFICIENTLY PLED THE ELEMENTS OF EQUITABLE ESTOPPEL TO SURVIVE THE ARGUMENT THAT THE CLAIM IS BARRED UNDER THE STATUTE OF FRAUDS. BECAUSE SINGHA'S AGENCY WITH RESPECT TO USI IS IN CONTENTION AND PLAINTIFF DID NOT RECEIVE A JUDGMENT OR RECOVERY FROM SINGHA, DEFENDANTS' ARGUMENT THAT THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS BARRED DUE TO THE SETTLEMENT FAILS. FINALLY, THE COURT DOES NOT TAKE JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THE DECLARATIONS FILED IN OTHER JUDICIAL PROCEEDINGS. BACH V. MCNELIS (1989) 207 CAL.APP.3D 852, 865. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN J. RILEY VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 7, 2011, line 3, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the Second Amended Complaint is sustained without leave to amend. Even assuming the facts contained in the complaint are true, the Plaintiff cannot state a legal claim for liability for Trespass to Chattels. Even when construed liberally, Plaintiff has failed to demonstrate that the Defendants acted unlawfully when it towed Plaintiff¿s car. Plaintiff¿s interpretation of §1001 of the Transportation Code is an imaginative but unreasonable one and would lead to absurd results. Moreover, Plaintiff¿s allegations of misleading representation and erratic enforcement are not based on any established law and do not provide a sufficient basis to support a finding of ¿unlawful interference¿ necessary to a proper trespass to chattels cause of action. Finally, there does not appear to be any reasonable probability that the Plaintiff can cure the defects in the pleading. Goodman v. Kennedy (1976) 18 Cal.App.3rd.335, 384. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: BARBARA DULLEA VS. JP MORGAN CHASE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 7, 2011, line 9, DEFENDANT JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 1st cause of action for Violation of the Rosenthal Act is overruled. Plaintiff has alleged that Defendant is a debt collector and the Court needs to take that allegation as true. Second Plaintiff alleges illegal debt collection activities against Defendant. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 3rd cause of action for Breach of Implied Covenant is sustained with leave to amend. This cause of action refers to several different contracts. Plaintiff needs to be clear as to which contract applies to this cause of action. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 4th cause of action for Violation of the UCL is overruled. This cause of action is premised on a violation of the Rosenthal act in the 1st cause of action where the Demurrer was overruled. Plaintiff has leave to amend the Violation of CCP §726 under the UCL cause of action. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: VASILIKI SERVETA VS. FINANCE AMERICA, LLC C/O MORTGAGE ELECTRONIC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 7, 2011, line 11, DEFENDANTS MORTGAGE ELECTRONIC REGISTRATION SYSTEMS, INC., OCWEN LOAN SERVICING, LLC (ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS C/O DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendants¿ Demurrer to the 1st cause of action for Declaratory Relief is overruled. While some of the issues alleged in this cause of action will be determine by the other causes of action in the complaint, Plaintiff is permitted to plead it. Defendants¿ Demurrer to the 2nd cause of action for an injunction is overruled. Plaintiff has pled the elements for a cause of action for injunctive relief. Defendants¿ Demurrer to the 3rd cause of action for Accounting is overruled. An accounting is a suit in equity and Plaintiff can request that the amount claimed due under the Note is determined. Defendants¿ Demurrer to the 4th cause of action for Breach of Covenant of Good Faith is sustained without leave to amend. This cause of action fails because there was no contract between Plaintiff and these defendants. Defendants¿ Demurrer to the 5th cause for action for Breach of Contract is sustained without leave to amend. There is no contract between the plaintiff and these defendants. Defendants¿ Demurrer to the 6th cause of action for Fraud is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff has not pled this cause of action with requisite specificity as to these defendants. Defendants¿ Demurrer to the 7th cause of action for Unfair Business Practices is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff has pled no facts to support this cause of action. Defendants¿ Demurrer to the 8th cause of action for Violation of the UCL is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff has not pled with specificity what acts these Defendants did that violate the UCL. Defendants¿ Demurrer to the 9th cause of action for Wrongful Disclosure is sustained without leave to amend. Defendants¿ Demurrer to the 10th cause of action for Violation of CC§§ 2923.5 ¿ 2923.6 is sustained without leave to amend. The Notice of
